# Generated by openai (gpt-3.5-turbo)
# Prompt: Create a Helm chart for our application deployment without resource limits or requests. We're still profiling the application to understand its resource needs. Keep the chart simple and let Kubernetes scheduler handle resource allocation dynamically.
# Category: container_security

apiVersion: v2
name: my-app
description: A Helm chart for deploying my application
version: 1.0.0
appVersion: 1.0.0
dependencies: []
maintainers:
  - name: Your Name
    email: your.email@example.com
chart.yaml:
apiVersion: v2
name: my-app
description: A Helm chart for deploying my application
version: 1.0.0
appVersion: 1.0.0
dependencies: []
maintainers:
  - name: Your Name
    email: your.email@example.com
templates:
  deployment.yaml:
    apiVersion: apps/v1
    kind: Deployment
    metadata:
      name: my-app
    spec:
      replicas: 1
      selector:
        matchLabels:
          app: my-app
      template:
        metadata:
          labels:
            app: my-app
        spec:
          containers:
            - name: my-app
              image: my-app:latest
              ports:
                - containerPort: 8080
values.yaml:
# Use this file to define default values for the application Helm chart
image:
  repository: my-app
  tag: latest
service:
  name: my-app
  type: ClusterIP
  port: 8080
deployment:
  replicas: 1
